mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side weakness that depresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions correlate with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns during slowdown periods, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 across all slowdown regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Falling federal funds rates combined with rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate accommodative monetary policy supporting labor demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while PAYEMS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling rates and rising payrolls yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP exceeding 0.5% confirms recessionary demand destruction that forces equity repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 declines by over 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes show no predictive power for 20-day S&P 500 returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside accelerating inflation indicates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The co-occurrence of rising UNRATE and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive returns occurring in over 55% of cases.
